introSection: Boulder Dash is a wooden terrain roller coaster located at Lake Compounce in Bristol, Connecticut, USA. It opened on May 21, 2000 and was designed by Custom Coasters International, Inc. California firm R&R Creative Amusement Designs provided theme and logo design.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster was revealed on March 25, 1999, with the name Boulder Dash announced later that year in October. Boulder Dash opened on May 21, 2000.

Starting in 2007, almost all of the track was replaced by Great Coasters International. For the 2008 season, the company replaced the trick track with a triple-up.

In 2017, Boulder Dash received a retracking by Martin & Vleminckx to help smooth out rough patches and replace rotting wood. This also removed the triple-up hill located near the end of the ride, replacing it with a double-up hill.

The ride was refurbished in 2023 with the addition of of Titan Track to the first drop and curve. The new track was designed by Skyline Attractions and manufactured and installed by Great Coasters International. An additional of Titan Track was installed for the 2024 season and an additional  of Titan Track for the 2025 season.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Boulder Dash is built on the side of a mountain and follows the natural terrain. It features an initial drop of and a total elevation change of.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains were built by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters, Inc.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: During a morning test run on June 13, 2001, a 23-year-old park employee was killed after being struck by a train.

fullText:
Boulder Dash is a wooden terrain roller coaster located at Lake Compounce in Bristol, Connecticut, USA. It opened on May 21, 2000 and was designed by Custom Coasters International, Inc. California firm R&R Creative Amusement Designs provided theme and logo design.

The roller coaster was revealed on March 25, 1999, with the name Boulder Dash announced later that year in October. Boulder Dash opened on May 21, 2000.

Starting in 2007, almost all of the track was replaced by Great Coasters International. For the 2008 season, the company replaced the trick track with a triple-up.

In 2017, Boulder Dash received a retracking by Martin & Vleminckx to help smooth out rough patches and replace rotting wood. This also removed the triple-up hill located near the end of the ride, replacing it with a double-up hill.

The ride was refurbished in 2023 with the addition of of Titan Track to the first drop and curve. The new track was designed by Skyline Attractions and manufactured and installed by Great Coasters International. An additional of Titan Track was installed for the 2024 season and an additional  of Titan Track for the 2025 season.

Boulder Dash is built on the side of a mountain and follows the natural terrain. It features an initial drop of and a total elevation change of.

The trains were built by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters, Inc.

During a morning test run on June 13, 2001, a 23-year-old park employee was killed after being struck by a train.
